task_completion.description: The CT Cranium DICOM dataset is loaded into the InVesalius application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius interface is open and displays 2D medical image slices (axial, coronal, sagittal) of a cranium/head.

task_completion.description: The InVesalius 3D Viewer background is configured to be white.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The background color of the 3D viewer pane in InVesalius is changed from its default color to solid white.

task_completion.description: A 3D surface of the skull is generated using the standard Bone preset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a skull is visible in the 3D viewer pane. The 'Bone' preset may be visibly selected in the surface generation panel on the left.

task_completion.description: The 3D skull is oriented into the four standard anatomical views (Anterior, Posterior, Left Lateral, Right Lateral).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D skull being rotated or snapped into distinct standard views (facing forward, backward, left, and right) within the InVesalius 3D viewer.

task_completion.description: Four PNG screenshots are exported to /home/ga/Documents/views/ with the correct filenames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Save dialogs, a file manager window, or terminal output confirming the creation of 'anterior.png', 'posterior.png', 'left.png', and 'right.png' in the '/home/ga/Documents/views/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius GUI to load data, configure the view, and export images.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with InVesalius menus, buttons, and the 3D viewer, rather than the agent bypassing the UI with a script.

integrity.description: The exported images are generated from the actual DICOM data in InVesalius.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D skull model matches the loaded DICOM slices and is seen inside the InVesalius application window before being exported.

integrity.description: The agent generated four distinct anatomical views, not duplicates.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer states or the final exported images show the skull from clearly different angles corresponding to anterior, posterior, left, and right.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is exactly four files: 'anterior.png', 'posterior.png', 'left.png', and 'right.png' located in '/home/ga/Documents/views'. The background of the 3D viewer and these exported images must be white (RGB 255, 255, 255 with a tolerance of ±5). The images must represent four distinct anatomical views of the skull generated from the CT Cranium (case 0051) dataset.
